description: Unified MCP gateway for registering upstream servers, authenticating clients, discovering tools, enforcing tool access policies, and routing tool calls through a single managed endpoint.

userStories.title: Add upstream server configuration model
userStories.description: As a platform administrator, I want a persistent model for upstream MCP server definitions so that the gateway can manage server metadata consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Add upstream server configuration entity or aggregate with fields: serverCode, name, baseUrl, transportType, authMode, enabled, timeoutMs, environment
Add repository port and infrastructure persistence model for create, update, list, enable, and disable operations
Duplicate serverCode within the same environment is rejected by the domain or application layer
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Expose admin API for upstream server lifecycle
userStories.description: As a platform administrator, I want admin endpoints to register and manage upstream MCP servers so that I can maintain gateway connectivity without code changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Add admin endpoints or commands for create, update, enable, disable, and list upstream server configurations
Validate required fields and reject invalid baseUrl or unsupported transportType values
Create and update responses return the persisted serverCode and enabled state
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add client credential authentication
userStories.description: As an AI application developer, I want to authenticate with a single gateway credential so that I do not manage separate credentials for each upstream server.
acceptanceCriteria: Support API Key and Bearer Token authentication at gateway entry points
Missing or invalid credentials return a standardized 401 or 403 response
Unauthenticated requests do not reach downstream application handlers
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Bootstrap request context and session identifiers
userStories.description: As a gateway operator, I want each request to carry a normalized context so that logs, errors, and downstream actions can be correlated reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate a unique requestId for every inbound request
Populate authenticated clientId and sessionId into a request context object after authentication succeeds
Include requestId in standardized error responses and request-scoped logs
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add tool access policy model
userStories.description: As a platform administrator, I want a persistent model for tool access policies so that tool permissions can be managed per client or client group.
acceptanceCriteria: Add policy model with subject type, subject id, tool identifier, decision, enabled flag, and optional reason fields
Add repository port and persistence operations for create, update, list, and disable policy records
Policy records support whitelist and blacklist decisions
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Evaluate tool access policy before invocation
userStories.description: As a platform administrator, I want the gateway to evaluate tool access policies before calling an upstream server so that restricted tools are blocked at the edge.
acceptanceCriteria: Evaluate whitelist or blacklist policy for the authenticated client or client group before route execution
Denied requests return a standardized forbidden response with an auditable deny reason
Policy changes take effect for new requests without restarting the service
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement upstream health check service
userStories.description: As a platform administrator, I want to track upstream server health so that unhealthy servers are excluded from new routing decisions.
acceptanceCriteria: Add health check application service that probes each enabled upstream server
Store or expose healthStatus and lastCheckedAt for each upstream server
Servers marked unhealthy are excluded from new route candidates
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fetch tool catalog from one upstream server
userStories.description: As a gateway developer, I want an adapter that reads tool metadata from one upstream MCP server so that the gateway can build a unified catalog.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an upstream adapter that fetches toolName, description, inputSchema, and serverCode from one healthy upstream server
Disabled, unhealthy, or unreachable upstream servers are skipped instead of producing a successful catalog entry
Tool identifiers preserve source uniqueness by combining serverCode with toolName
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Expose aggregated tool discovery endpoint
userStories.description: As an AI application developer, I want one endpoint that returns all available tools so that I can discover capabilities without querying each upstream server separately.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a gateway discovery endpoint that returns the merged tool list from enabled and healthy upstream servers
Each returned tool includes toolName, serverCode, description, and inputSchema
Tools from unavailable upstream servers do not appear in the response
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add tool route selection service
userStories.description: As a gateway developer, I want a route selector for tool requests so that each tool call resolves to the correct upstream server deterministically.
acceptanceCriteria: Add route selection logic based on explicit tool identifier or configured binding between tool and upstream server
Unknown or ambiguous tool identifiers return a standardized not found or conflict response before upstream invocation
Route selection only considers enabled and healthy upstream servers
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement upstream tool invocation adapter
userStories.description: As a gateway developer, I want an adapter that forwards tool calls to an upstream MCP server so that execution can happen behind a consistent gateway contract.
acceptanceCriteria: Forward normalized tool invocation payloads to the selected upstream server
Map upstream success, timeout, 4xx, 5xx, and protocol errors into a standardized gateway response model
Pass through required tool arguments without dropping defined input fields
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add gateway tool invocation flow
userStories.description: As an AI application developer, I want to call a tool through one gateway endpoint so that authentication, policy checks, routing, and invocation happen in a single request flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a gateway tool invocation endpoint or command handler that runs authentication, request context setup, access policy evaluation, route selection, and upstream invocation in order
Requests stop at the failing stage and do not proceed to later stages after authentication, policy, or route errors
Successful and failed responses include requestId and use the standardized gateway response model
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Record audit logs for tool calls
userStories.description: As a platform administrator, I want audit logs for tool calls so that I can trace client behavior, routing decisions, and failures.
acceptanceCriteria: Log requestId, clientId, toolName, serverCode, latency, and final status for every tool invocation attempt
Denied requests and upstream failures are logged with the same correlation fields
Audit log records are emitted from the gateway invocation flow without changing the external response contract
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Expose admin upstream status and refresh actions
userStories.description: As a platform administrator, I want to query upstream status and trigger refresh actions so that I can diagnose and recover server availability quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an admin status endpoint that returns serverCode, enabled, healthStatus, and lastCheckedAt for all upstream servers
Add an admin action to trigger health refresh for one upstream server or all enabled upstream servers
Status results reflect the most recent completed health check execution
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
